subject: Biology
title: Prokaryotes vs Eukaryotes
description: A 1-minute de-risking session on Prokaryotes vs Eukaryotes.

hook.text: Every cell on Earth belongs to one of two groups. The split happened billions of years ago.

buildup.text: Prokaryotes are simple: no nucleus, no organelles. Bacteria and archaea are prokaryotes.

discovery.text: Eukaryotes have a nucleus and specialized compartments. Plants, animals, and fungi are all eukaryotic.

twist.text: Prokaryotes ruled Earth alone for 2 billion years before the first eukaryote appeared.

climax.text: One cell likely swallowed another, creating mitochondria and launching complex life.

punchline.text: Two designs. One ancient, one revolutionary.

quiz.question: What makes eukaryotic cells different from prokaryotic cells?
options: They have a nucleus and organelles
They can only be found in animals
